subject: Biology
title: Seeds: Life in a Package
description: A fast breakdown of Seeds: Life in a Package for builders.

hook.text: A seed is a plant embryo wrapped in a protective coat with a packed lunch of stored nutrients.

buildup.text: Seeds can survive drought, cold, and even digestion by animals—waiting for the right moment.

discovery.text: A 2,000-year-old date palm seed found at Masada was germinated successfully in a lab.

twist.text: Coconuts are seeds that float across oceans. Dandelion seeds fly on the wind.

climax.text: Seed banks now store millions of varieties as insurance against crop failure and extinction.

punchline.text: Tiny packages, ancient patience, endless potential.

quiz.question: How old was the date palm seed successfully germinated from Masada?
